CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE VICINITY OF ELLENTON OMEGA ROAD AND TRAVELED NORTHEASTWARD AROUND 3 MILES WHILE SKIPPING UP AND DOWN BEFORE LIFTING PERMANENTLY NEAR THE COLQUITT/COOK COUNTY BORDER JUST PAST LAWYER SUMNER ROAD BUT BEFORE CROSSING I-75. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO BARNS...IRRIGATION PIVOTS...AND SOME ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH OF AROUND 50 YARDS.
SUSPECTED TORNADO BASED ON MEDIA REPORTS EAST SOUTHEAST OF OMEGA IN COLQUITT COUNTY. SUSPECTED TORNADO PATH IS ESIMATED TO HAVE CONTINUED EASTWARD ALONG THE TIFT-COOK COUNTY LINE NEAR INTERSTATE 75.

SURVEY CONFIRMS EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR COUNTY ROAD 9 SOUTH OF SLOCOMB AROUND 112 PM CST AND MOVED EASTWARD AROUND 1.23 MILES BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COTTON BOX RD AND DAVIS MILL RD AROUND 114 PM CST. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH WITH ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES AND A BARN DESTROYED. MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 95 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH AROUND 50 YARDS.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE. 1419 S COUNTY RD 9. POWER LINES DOWN AND SOME STRUCURAL DAMAGE TO A HOUSE AND BARN.

A SECOND EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 103 AND TINDIL CREEK RD TO THE SOUTH OF THE FIRST TORNADO AROUND 114 PM CST. THIS TORNADO TRAVELED EAST-NORTHEASTWARD AROUND ONE-THIRD OF A MILE ACROSS TINDIL CREEK RD BEFORE LIFTING AROUND 115 PM CST. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG ITS PATH. THE PATH WIDTH WAS ESTIMATED AROUND 30 YARDS WITH MAX WINDS AROUND 95 MPH.

AN NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR VADA ROAD AND MOVED NORTHEASTWARD AROUND 3.37 MILES WITH INTERMITTENT DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING NEAR 31.07N AND 84.38W AT AROUND 835 AM. THE MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH WITH A WIDTH AROUND 25 YARDS. AN ATTACHED GARAGE OF A HOME WAS DESTROYED. A CAR WAS ALSO FLIPPED WITH A FARM EQUIPMENT SHED DESTROYED.
